title: The Skin of the South
notes_on_eligibility: Ishiro Honda's 2nd feature (Toho). Researchers explicitly studying soil/volcanic-ash (Shirasu) stability; lead is a geologist-figure. Confirm exact on-screen term (Japanese 'geologist' vs 'soil researcher') on viewing.
notes_on_characterization: Cassandra narrative: warns village + lumber/development interests of landslide risk; ignored on two fronts (villagers fear relocation; baron's deforestation worsens it). Disaster arrives. Honda's anti-capitalist / man-vs-environment theme, pre-Godzilla. Colleague Takayama and Tokyo-dispatched researcher Sadae Miura also present. VERIFY survival of Ohno + whether anyone dies in the landslide finale.

title: The Skin of the South
notes_on_eligibility: Second research-team member.
notes_on_characterization: Colleague of Ohno on the soil/stability survey. Confirm role + fate on viewing.

title: Letter Never Sent
notes_on_eligibility: Kalatozov/Urusevsky. Four-person geological diamond expedition explicitly central. Soviet survival epic.
notes_on_characterization: Expedition leader + the 'letter never sent' writer (Smoktunovsky). Forest fire then winter traps the party; nearly all perish. Self-sacrifice-for-the-State framing (find diamonds to enrich USSR). Survival ambiguous/tragic — VERIFY exactly who lives; final shots debated. Classic Soviet BUILDER tradition the codebook anticipates.

title: Letter Never Sent
notes_on_eligibility: Female geologist — notable early non-Anglo woman in the role.
notes_on_characterization: Tatyana Samoylova. Love triangle (with Andrei/Sergei). Predates Sturkell's 'first female geologist' (1957 Anglo); useful data point for the female-representation thread. VERIFY fate.

title: Letter Never Sent
notes_on_eligibility: Third of four.
notes_on_characterization: Livanov. Tanya's love interest. VERIFY fate.

title: Letter Never Sent
notes_on_eligibility: Fourth of four.
notes_on_characterization: Urbansky. Jealous/insecure; physical altercation with Andrei. VERIFY fate. (Guide Konstantin is NOT a geologist — exclude.)

title: Papaya: Love Goddess of the Cannibals
notes_on_eligibility: Joe D'Amato exploitation/horror. Synopsis says 'geologists'; on-screen the named lead Vincent is voiced as working on the nuclear-plant project — geologist/engineer slippage. FLAG term ambiguity; confirm on viewing whether 'geologo' is used.
notes_on_characterization: Maurice Poli. Colonial extractor archetype: dismisses local woman's capacity to understand the reactor project; treats island as disposable. Picked off by Papaya's seduction-killings. Strong anti-colonial / eco inversion (natives as agents, not victims). Sara (Sirpa Lane) is a JOURNALIST, not a geologist — exclude. VERIFY Vincent's death + how many team members are geologists vs engineers.

title: Focusing on the Turquoise Mountain
notes_on_eligibility: Czech-Mongol co-production (normalization-era 'friendship' film). Explicitly Czechoslovak + Mongolian geologists prospecting copper.
notes_on_characterization: Langmiler. Author of the research plan; bets on folk legend of the 'Turquoise Mountain' (turquoise as copper indicator). Initially doubted by colleague Mares; ultimately vindicated. Comedic adventure tone; reads as state-socialist scientific-cooperation agitka. BUILDER/EXTRACTOR overtones (resource expedition for the bloc).

title: Focusing on the Turquoise Mountain
notes_on_eligibility: Expedition leader, skeptic.
notes_on_characterization: Hlusicka. Head of the Czech expedition; opposes Pudil's legend-driven method, then must concede. Rationalist foil.

title: Focusing on the Turquoise Mountain
notes_on_eligibility: Geoindustria official.
notes_on_characterization: Vetrovec. Branch director of state enterprise Geoindustria; bureaucratic/satirical note (dusting his Skoda etc.). Confirm whether credited specifically as geologist/engineer.

title: Where the Green Ants Dream
notes_on_eligibility: RESOLVES the codebook's 'needs investigation': YES, an explicitly named geologist protagonist. Herzog docu-fiction on Aboriginal land rights vs uranium mining.
notes_on_characterization: Bruce Spence. Company geologist (Ayers Mining) surveying for uranium with explosives; becomes intermediary and grows sympathetic to the Aboriginal cause but does not stop the machine. The EXTRACTOR-turned-conscience; ends in 'resigned wanderings into the void.' Prime anti-colonial / land-relationship case diverging hard from the Anglo template. (Cole = mining engineer, Ferguson = exec — not geologists.)

title: A Place in the World
notes_on_eligibility: Aristarain. Explicitly 'geological engineer'/geologist (Hans). Acclaimed; Oscar-nominated then disqualified on submission technicality.
notes_on_characterization: Jose Sacristan. Jaded surveyor ostensibly looking for oil, actually scouting a hydro dam for a multinational/local patron that would displace the peasants. Bonds with the family, TEACHES geology at the village school (rare positive pedagogical portrayal) yet serves displacement. Morally ambiguous extractor; post-dictatorship Argentine idealism-vs-pragmatism. Land/landscape relationship central.

title: The Wave
notes_on_eligibility: Norway's first big-budget disaster film. Geologist protagonist explicit.
notes_on_characterization: Kristoffer Joner. Cassandra at the Akerneset monitoring station; spots instability, alarm held off for tourist season; megatsunami hits Geiranger. Heroic — saves family; ~250 die. Bittersweet survival. Same character continues into The Quake. Real Akerneset hazard basis.

title: Beyond Sleep
notes_on_eligibility: METADATA FLAG: primary language is DUTCH (adaptation of W.F. Hermans' 'Nooit meer slapen'), not Norwegian — CSV lists original_language blank/'no'; correct to 'nl'. Shot in Finnmark.
notes_on_characterization: Reinout Scholten van Aschat. Geology student/2nd-gen geologist chasing late father's meteorite-crater theory; ridiculed by Norwegian Prof. Nummedal; insomnia + midnight sun + paranoia toward companions (Arne, Qvigstad, Mikkelsen). Psychological, not disaster. VERIFY whether companion Arne's death (a fall, as in the novel) is retained on screen — if so add a row + possible 2nd geologist fatality (ACCIDENT/GEOLOGICAL). Ending: survives 'on the edge of madness,' ambiguous.

title: The Quake
notes_on_eligibility: Sequel to The Wave; same geologist protagonist.
notes_on_characterization: Kristoffer Joner. PTSD/survivor-guilt recluse; investigates colleague's death, predicts Oslo quake, ignored again (wolf-cried), vindicated catastrophically. Heroic; survives. Note: same character coded in two films — decide whether to count once or per-film for Sturkell-style tallies.

title: The Quake
notes_on_eligibility: Inciting 'found dead' colleague — a second geologist.
notes_on_characterization: Deceased fellow geologist whose tunnel-collapse death and research set the plot moving; he was right about the seismic danger. Found dead (death not depicted as central on-screen event). Vindicated posthumously. VERIFY name spelling/credit.

notes_on_characterization: Conceptually rich (geo-acoustics, post-eruption island) and the 'acoustic geologist' is a striking non-Anglo INVESTIGATOR archetype worth a footnote — but per codebook criteria (theatrical narrative fiction) it is INELIGIBLE as a non-theatrical doc-short. Flag as borderline / qualitative-mention candidate rather than dataset entry.
